LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (AS TO PLAINTIFF JAMES LEWIS' CLAIM FOR RETALIATION). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. THIS IS NOT AN IMPROPER MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION BECAUSE THE ISSUES RAISED IN THIS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WERE NOT RAISED IN THE PREVIOUS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. IN THE FIRST MOTION, AS TO THE RETALIATION CLAIM, DEFENDANT DID NOT MOVE ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF SUFFERED AND ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. ANY CLAIM OF ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION DUE TO THE 2005 TRANSFER FAILS BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAD NOT ENGAGED IN PROTECTED ACTIVITY. AS FOR THE CHANGE THAT RESULTED AFTER PLAINTIFF ATTENDED CHIEF FONG'S DEPOSITION, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT HE SUFFERED AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION WHICH IS REASONABLY LIKELY TO ADVERSELY AND MATERIALLY AFFECT HIS JOB PERFORMANCE OR OPPORTUNITY FOR ADVANCEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S JOB WAS CHANGED FROM RECORDS DESTRUCTION TO CLERICAL WORK. PLAINTIFF DID NOT RECEIVE A REDUCTION IN SALARY OR BENEFITS. PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT HE CAN NO LONGER RECEIVE OUTSIDE TRAINING WHICH AFFECTS HIS PROMOTION POTENTIAL IS WITHOUT MERIT BECAUSE THIS RELATES TO HIS INITIAL TRANSFER TO THE RECORD ROOM. (SEE THOMAS V. DEPT. OF CORRECTIONS (2000) 77 CAL. APP. 4TH 507; MCRAE V. DEPT. OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION (2006) 142 CAL. APP. 4TH 377). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
